scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. The real documented API lives at https://payments.pabbly.com/api/v1 (Basic auth: apikey/secretkey); the live docs_url now redirects to a marketing-only SPA with no endpoint content behind it (confirmed by fetching it directly), so the full endpoint list below was recovered from a Wayback Machine capture of the same docs page (2021-11-28, https://web.archive.org/web/20211128152246/https://www.pabbly.com/subscriptions/api/) cross-checked against the legacy connector's own base_url constant. This bundle keeps its 4 pre-existing GET-list streams' legacy-parity base_url/paths unchanged (an unannounced base-URL host migration is out of scope for a capability-expansion pass; see docs.md Known limits) and adds every remaining practical write action plus 2 additional list endpoints (products, and a superseded per-product plans view) as new Pass-B coverage. Every single-resource-by-id GET (customer/{id}, subscription/{id}, product/{id}, plan/{id}, invoice/{id}, addon/{id}, addoncategory/{id}) is excluded as duplicate_of its corresponding list endpoint: this dialect has no per-record dynamic-id enumeration source for these (no config-list of ids, no parent listing endpoint feeding a fan_out), and each list endpoint already returns every field its single-resource counterpart does.

excluded.reason: single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source (no fan_out ids_from) and the customers list stream already returns every field this endpoint does
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the subscriptions list stream already returns every field this endpoint does

single-resource fetch keyed by subscription_id; no dynamic-id enumeration source, and it is a narrow scheduled-change sub-view of the same subscription this bundle already reads in full via the subscriptions stream
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the products list stream already returns every field this endpoint does
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the plans list stream already returns every field this endpoint does

narrower per-product view of records the plans stream already returns in full (every plan record carries its own product_id field, so the plans stream is a strict superset of this endpoint's results across all products)

list-all-coupons-for-a-product is keyed by product_id with no bundle-level product-id source to iterate; would need a fan_out over the products stream's ids, a natural but not-yet-modeled future increment rather than today's scope
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the invoices list stream already returns every field this endpoint does

no dynamic invoice-id enumeration source (invoices are only ever read per-customer, not via a flat orderable id list this bundle can fan out over); a genuine capability gap tracked for a future fan_out-driven increment
same invoice-id enumeration gap as Record Payment Invoice above
per-customer sub-resource list with no bundle-level customer-id source to fan out over economically; tracked for a future fan_out increment
per-product sub-resource list with no bundle-level product-id source to fan out over economically; same gap shape as the coupon list above

a checkout/thank-you-page hosted-token verification callback for a customer-facing storefront redirect flow, not a reverse-ETL warehouse write; has no meaningful place in either a stream or a writes.json action
per-customer sub-resource list with no bundle-level customer-id source to fan out over economically; tracked for a future fan_out increment
per-customer sub-resource list with no bundle-level customer-id source to fan out over economically; tracked for a future fan_out increment
mints a customer-facing self-service login link/token for the Pabbly client portal; a session-token mint, not a data mutation with any reverse-ETL analog
returns pre-aggregated dashboard summary statistics (total_sales, subscription_count, etc.), not individual billing records; no natural primary key or stable per-record identity for a stream

an ad hoc reporting aggregate over a caller-chosen product/interval filter with no primary key or stable record identity across calls (re-running with the same interval returns the current snapshot, not a stable append-only collection) — a reporting export, not a syncable stream
same reporting-aggregate shape as Create Net-Revenue Status above
per-plan sub-resource list with no bundle-level plan-id source to fan out over economically; tracked for a future fan_out increment
single-resource fetch by id (docs label it "List Single Addon" but the real HTTP request example is a plain GET by addon_id); no dynamic-id enumeration source

per-product sub-resource list with no bundle-level product-id source to fan out over economically; tracked for a future fan_out increment (would pair naturally with a products-id fan_out once modeled)
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the per-product addon-category list already returns every field this endpoint does for products this bundle could enumerate
per-product sub-resource list with no bundle-level product-id source to fan out over economically; same gap shape as the addons list above
